definition: Copper and commodity positioning is historically crowded — BofA FMS overweight at +2.1σ, $58B in 2025 ETF inflows, 13/15 sell-side desks structurally bullish — creating near-term unwind risk. Casualty evidence is mounting (Pimco -26% YTD, Conductor wound down, Millennium ~$1.5B loss).
evidence: BofA FMS commodity overweight at +2.1σ (up from +1.8σ)
$58B in 2025 commodity ETF inflows, $11.4B in Jan-Feb 2026
CFTC copper net longs at 48K contracts (declining but elevated)
Combined equity + commodity allocation at 76%, last seen early 2022
Casualty evidence: Pimco -26% YTD, Conductor wound down, Millennium ~$1.5B loss
open_questions: Does the 2000s precedent (supercycle running years after consensus despite crowding) apply?
Is +2.1σ historically in the zone where drawdowns are deeper and more prolonged?
